sections.name: Giant Bone Skipper
sections.text: While less common than the smaller variety, giant bone skippers are the stuff of nightmares for morticians and graveyard keepers.

family.name: Bone Skipper

sections.name: Megafauna Scavengers
sections.text: Bone skippers eat and breed within the bones of deceased megafauna, such as elephants and teraphants. In areas where their carcasses are rare or quickly disposed of, bone skippers are virtually unknown. Sufficiently large undead creatures, like zombie hulks, might harbor a bone skipper swarm that buzzes around them, defending their gruesome “lairs.”

family.text: Orange-headed bone skippers feast on the flesh of the dead, particularly seeking out the marrow in the bones of large carcasses. These strange flies are named for the way they hop about on rotting bodies as they feed, causing the corpses to move and appear as if alive.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Animal
traits.text: An animal is a creature with a relatively low intelligence. It typically doesn’t have an Intelligence ability modifier over –4, can’t speak languages, and can’t be trained in Intelligence-based skills.

interaction_abilities.name: Bonesense
interaction_abilities.text: A giant bone skipper identifies bones and creatures with a skeletal system in the listed range.

ability.name: Leaping Charge
ability.text: The giant bone skipper Leaps up to 20 feet. It then makes a mandibles Strike, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to its attack roll.

ability.context: Marrow rot affects only creatures with a skeletal system.
ability.name: Marrow Rot

traits.name: Disease
traits.text: An effect with this trait applies one or more diseases. A disease is typically an affliction.

universal_monster_ability.name: Darkvision
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-level *darkness* spell, block normal darkvision. A monster with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.
